title: hieracium prenanthoides subsp. juvonis
intro: Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. juvonis, also known as the hawkweed, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, and is traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Hieracium prenanthoides subsp. juvonis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
